description:
You are acting as a FinOps analyst who has been asked to tighten the experimental cloud-lab firewall configuration while still allowing Prometheus to scrape metrics on TCP port 9090.

Initial setup already in place:
1. Directory structure  
   • /home/user/config/ (contains the current firewall rules file)  
   • /home/user/logs/   (intended for all change-tracking log files)

2. Existing file  
   • /home/user/config/firewall.rules  
     Current contents (exactly three lines, **including the final blank line**):  
       -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT  
       -A INPUT -j DROP  
   

Goal:
A. Modify /home/user/config/firewall.rules so that it allows inbound TCP traffic on port 9090 **in addition to** the existing SSH rule on port 22, while keeping the final DROP rule last.  
   • The file must end up containing **exactly** the following three lines and a trailing newline:  
       -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT  
       -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9090 -j ACCEPT  
       -A INPUT -j DROP  

B. Create the change-tracking log /home/user/logs/firewall_actions.log.  
   • If the file already exists, append to it; otherwise create it.  
   • Write exactly two log entries (one per line) using the format:  
       YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | ACTION | DETAIL  
     where the timestamp is the current system time when the action is executed.  
     Expected ACTION values for this task:  
       ALLOW_PORT    (detail must be “9090”)  
       VERIFY_RULES  (detail must be “firewall.rules updated”)  
   • Example layout (timestamps will differ in the automatic check):  
       2025-02-18 14:37:02 | ALLOW_PORT | 9090  
       2025-02-18 14:37:03 | VERIFY_RULES | firewall.rules updated  

C. Do **not** change permissions or ownership of any existing files or directories.  
D. Do not use sudo or require elevated privileges. All modifications happen only to the text files inside /home/user.  
E. When you finish, the automated grader will verify:  
   1. The exact three-line content of /home/user/config/firewall.rules (with a trailing newline).  
   2. The existence of /home/user/logs/firewall_actions.log containing two newline-terminated entries matching the format above.  
   3. That the first log entry includes “ALLOW_PORT | 9090” and the second includes “VERIFY_RULES | firewall.rules updated”.

The grader is strict about whitespace and order, so follow the line layouts exactly. Good luck!
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent begins:

Directory structure (exists with 755 permissions):
/home/user/config/
/home/user/logs/

File 1: /home/user/config/firewall.rules (644 permissions)
Content (including a terminating newline):
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -j DROP

File 2: /home/user/logs/firewall_actions.log
—This file is NOT present at start.

Expected final filesystem state after a correct solution:

/home/user/config/firewall.rules (contents must be **exactly**):
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9090 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -j DROP

/home/user/logs/firewall_actions.log
Contains exactly two lines, each ending with a newline. Line-1 regex:
^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} \| ALLOW_PORT \| 9090\n$
Line-2 regex:
^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} \| VERIFY_RULES \| firewall\.rules updated\n$

The first line must appear before (chronologically earlier than) the second line.
